findings.title: Request With Http
findings.description: Detected a request using 'http://'. This request will be unencrypted, and attackers could listen into traffic on the network and be able to obtain sensitive information. Use 'https://' instead.
findings.cweId: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information

fix.description: Review the code and apply the appropriate security fix

findings.attackStory: Security misconfiguration at scripts/osm_mq_buildings.py weakens the application's security posture and may enable other attacks.

findings.title: Missing RLS: public.xp_config
findings.description: Table public.xp_config does not have Row Level Security enabled. This means any authenticated user can access all rows.
findings.evidence: CREATE TABLE public.xp_config -- Missing ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY
findings.attackStory: Without RLS on xp_config, any authenticated user can read, modify, or delete all rows in this table, potentially accessing other users' data.

fix.description: Enable RLS and add appropriate policies
